operation.label: Control operation
operation.q1: Has the control been executed consistently according to the procedure during the recent period?
operation.q2: Can you provide evidence demonstrating consistent execution of the control (e.g. logs, reports, screenshots)?
operation.q3: Are any deviations or exceptions from the procedure documented and approved?

monitoring.label: Monitoring and improvement
monitoring.q1: Is the effectiveness of this control monitored and reported to the control owner on a periodic basis?
monitoring.q2: Has the control been evaluated within the past year to confirm that it remains effective and efficient?
monitoring.q3: Have improvement actions been identified and implemented based on evaluations or detected deviations?

create.heading: Create encrypted backup
create.description: Creates a new backup of the database. If an encryption key is configured via the environment, it will be used automatically. You can also provide a custom key below.
create.key_configured: Encryption key configured via environment — will be used unless you provide a custom key below.
create.no_key: No encryption key configured in the environment. Enter a custom key below to encrypt this backup, or leave it empty to create an unencrypted backup.
create.custom_key_placeholder: Leave empty to use the configured environment key
create.custom_key_help_env: Leave empty to use the key from BACKUP_ENCRYPTION_KEY. Enter or generate a different key to override for this backup only.
create.custom_key_help_no_env: No environment key is configured. Enter a key or click Generate to create one. Leave empty for an unencrypted backup.

key.warning_body: This key is shown once. Save it in a secure location before leaving this page. Without it, you cannot restore encrypted backups.

restore.title: Restore a backup
restore.heading: Restore a backup
restore.subtitle: Upload a backup file to queue it for restore.
restore.key_help: If the backup is encrypted, enter the key used when it was created. If left empty, the configured encryption key will be tried automatically.
restore.key_placeholder: Leave empty to use configured key
restore.decrypt_failed: Decryption failed. Please verify the encryption key and try again.

index.title: CMMI Maturity Assessment
index.subtitle: Assess your controls against CMMI maturity levels (2-5).

history.title: Assessment History
history.delete_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete this historical version? This action cannot be undone.

maturity.title: Maturity Assessment
maturity.evidence_placeholder: Link to policy or describe implementation...
maturity.reset_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete this assessment? All data including answers and notes will be lost.
maturity.assessment_permission_denied: You do not have permission to perform this action.
maturity.target_level_help: Select the intended maturity level to filter relevant requirements.

req1.text: Is the process planned and executed in accordance with policy?
req1.help: Verify if there is a documented policy governing this process and evidence of execution.

req2.text: Are skilled people providing adequate resources to perform the process?
req2.help: Check for resource allocation records and training certifications of personnel.

req3.text: Is the process monitored and controlled?
req3.help: Look for status reports, logs, or dashboard metrics regarding process performance.

req1.text: Is the process tailored from the organization's set of standard processes?
req1.help: Ensure the process is not ad-hoc but derived from the organizational standards with approved local customizations.

req2.text: Is the process maintained and improved based on organizational process assets?
req2.help: Verify contributions back to the organizational asset library (lessons learned, artifacts).

req3.text: Are process experiences contributed to the organizational process assets?
req3.help: Check for post-mortem reports or improvement suggestions logged in the central repository.

req1.text: Are quantitative objectives for quality and process performance established?
req1.help: Look for specific numeric targets (KPIs) defined for this process.

req2.text: Is the sub-process selected for quantitative management statistically managed?
req2.help: Check for control charts or statistical analysis of process variations.

req3.text: Can the process performance be predicted quantitatively?
req3.help: Evidence of predictive modeling or forecasting based on historical performance data.

req1.text: Are process improvements based on a quantitative understanding of the process's objectives?
req1.help: Improvements should be driven by data analysis, not just qualitative feedback.

req2.text: Is continuous process improvement enabled by quantitative feedback?
req2.help: Check for a feedback loop where metrics directly trigger improvement initiatives.

req3.text: Are the effects of process improvements measured and evaluated?
req3.help: Verify that after an improvement is applied, its impact is measured against the baseline.

index.title: Security Tools
index.subtitle: A collection of standalone security and compliance tools.

dashboard.title: Threat Models
dashboard.subtitle: STRIDE-LM based threat modeling workspace.
dashboard.no_models: No threat models found. Create your first one.

model_form.help_scope: Describe what systems and assets are in scope.
model_form.bia_import_hint: Select a Business Impact Analysis to automatically add its components as assets. Leave blank for a standalone threat model.

help.name: A short, descriptive name for the asset (e.g. "Customer database", "Payment API").
help.asset_type: The category that best describes this asset. Component: a process, service, or software element (e.g. web server, microservice). Data Flow: data in transit between components (e.g. API call, message queue). Trust Boundary: a boundary where trust levels change (e.g. DMZ, network perimeter, user/kernel boundary). External Entity: a person or system outside your control that interacts with the model (e.g. end user, third-party API). Data Store: data at rest (e.g. database, file share, cache).
help.description: Optional details about the asset: what it does, who owns it, where it resides, or why it is in scope.
help.order: Controls the display order of assets in lists. Lower numbers appear first.

help.stride_category: Select the STRIDE-LM category that best describes the nature of this threat. Spoofing (identity), Tampering (data), Repudiation (actions), Information Disclosure (exposure), Denial of Service (availability), Elevation of Privilege (access), Lateral Movement (spreading).
help.asset_id: The specific asset this scenario targets. Link it to an asset from the model to build a complete attack surface overview.
help.title: A concise, descriptive name for this threat scenario (e.g. "Unauthenticated API access", "SQL injection on login form").
help.description: A narrative description of the threat: what happens, who is involved, and why it matters.
help.threat_actor: Who or what initiates this threat? Examples: external attacker, malicious insider, automated bot, nation-state.
help.attack_vector: Describe the technical path or method an attacker would use to exploit this threat (e.g. network request, phishing email, physical access).
help.preconditions: List any conditions that must be true for this attack to succeed (e.g. valid credentials required, public endpoint exposed, unpatched library).
help.impact_description: Describe the real-world consequences if this scenario is realised: data loss, service downtime, regulatory breach, reputational damage, etc.
help.likelihood: How probable is it that this threat will materialise? 1 = Rare, 2 = Unlikely, 3 = Possible, 4 = Likely, 5 = Almost certain.
help.impact_score: How severe would the consequences be if this scenario occurs? 1 = Negligible, 2 = Minor, 3 = Moderate, 4 = Significant, 5 = Critical.
help.residual_likelihood: After controls are applied, how likely is this threat to still materialise? Leave blank if controls have not yet been assessed.
help.residual_impact: After controls are applied, how severe would the impact still be? Leave blank if controls have not yet been assessed.
help.treatment: How will this risk be handled? Accept (tolerate the risk), Mitigate (reduce likelihood or impact), Transfer (e.g. insure or outsource), or Avoid (eliminate the activity).
help.status: The current lifecycle stage of this scenario: Identified (logged), Analysed (assessed), Mitigated (controls in place), or Closed (accepted or resolved).
help.owner_id: The person accountable for monitoring and resolving this threat scenario.
help.csa_control_ids: Link one or more CSA controls that address or mitigate this scenario. Hold Ctrl / Cmd to select multiple.

scenario_detail.confirm_delete: Are you sure you want to delete this scenario?

flash.risk_sync_skipped: Scenario not synced: treatment must be Mitigate and status must be active.

help.name: A short, descriptive name for this threat.

poam.title: Plan of Action & Milestones
poam.delete_confirm: Delete this POA&M item and its milestones?

poam_form.resources_hint: Budget, personnel, tools, or other resources needed to accomplish this task.

help.authorization_boundary: Describe the system boundary: which components, networks, and data stores are included in the authorization scope.
help.laws_regulations: List applicable laws, regulations, and standards (e.g. GDPR, NIS2, ISO 27001, BIO). Include article references where relevant.
help.plan_completion_date: Target date for completing all sections of this SSP.
help.plan_approval_date: Date on which this SSP was formally reviewed and approved.
help.monitoring_kpis_kris: Key performance indicators (KPIs) and key risk indicators (KRIs) that measure the security posture of this system.
help.monitoring_what: Specify the controls, log sources, metrics, and events that are continuously monitored.
help.monitoring_who: Name the roles, teams, or individuals responsible for carrying out monitoring activities.
help.monitoring_tools: List the platforms, tools, or services used for monitoring (e.g. SIEM, IDS, vulnerability scanners).
help.monitoring_frequency: How often is monitoring performed? (e.g. Continuously, Daily, Monthly, Quarterly)
help.system_name: Name of the external or third-party system connected to this system.
help.owning_organization: Organization that owns or operates the connected system.
help.agreement_type: The formal agreement type that governs this interconnection (e.g. MOU, ISA).
help.data_direction: Direction of data flow between the two systems.
help.security_contact: Name or contact details of the security representative for the connected system.
help.notes: Additional context about this interconnection, including data types exchanged or security constraints.
help.implementation_status: Current state of implementation for this control.
help.responsible_entity: Team, role, or individual responsible for implementing and maintaining this control.
help.implementation_statement: Describe how this control is implemented. Be specific about measures, tools, and processes in place.
help.weakness_description: Describe the finding, gap, or weakness to be addressed. Reference the relevant control or audit finding where possible.
help.resources_required: Budget, personnel time, tools, or other resources required to complete this remediation.
help.point_of_contact: Name or role of the person accountable for completing this remediation.
help.scheduled_completion: Target date for completing the remediation of this weakness.
help.status: Current remediation status of this POA&M item.
